page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features a shared Intel x86_64 CPU with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads per core) operating at 2.0 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU (0.125 allocation) providing 2 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between general-purpose compute and specialized workloads. The shared CPU architecture results in weak multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3465.56 and a PassMark CPU score of 3668.28. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, with large-block read speeds at 27.11 GB/sec and write speeds at 19.81 GB/sec. However, the hardware accelerator delivers strong results in specific tasks; LLM inference prompt processing for a small 135M model reaches a top-tier speed of 7078.28 tokens/sec, while text generation for the same model averages 233.47 tokens/sec.

This instance offers an entry-level entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing fractional hardware resources. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and limited 2 GB VRAM, which restricts the instance from handling large-scale datasets or heavy parallel CPU tasks, as reflected in the poor PassMark database operations score of 989.35. It is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small-scale model testing, basic video transcoding, or educational GPU workloads rather than high-throughput database hosting or intensive multi-threaded computing.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance configured with 2 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD. It features a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU (0.125 allocation) with 2 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks indicate weak multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 3668.28 and large-block memory read speeds of 27.11 GB/sec. However, the GPU accelerator enables top-tier prompt processing speeds of 7078.28 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models. This instance represents a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated computing, trading off raw CPU power and VRAM capacity for budget-friendly resource access. It is suited for lightweight machine learning inference, small model prototyping, and basic parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.125 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB VRAM for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 0.125 NVIDIA A16 GPU with 2 GB VRAM.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for entry-level machine learning inference and prototyping.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB VRAM
2 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 8.0 GB RAM
Top-tier prompt processing for small LLM models
Suited for lightweight machine learning inference and model testing
